channel.title: NPR Topics: Economy
channel.description: NPR news on the U.S. and world economy, the World Bank, and Federal Reserve. Commentary on economic trends. Subscribe to NPR Economy podcasts and RSS feeds.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: The average U.S. gas price is now $4 a gallon. What does that mean for the economy?
item.description: The average national price of a gallon of gas has passed the $4 mark for the first time in more than three years.
item.content:encoded: The average national price of a gallon of gas has passed the $4 mark for the first time in more than three years.
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Gas crosses $4 a gallon in the U.S. for the first time in 3 years
item.description: The war with Iran has driven up gas prices at a time when affordability is high on people's minds.
item.content:encoded:
The war with Iran has driven up gas prices at a time when affordability is high on people's minds.

(Image credit: Ronaldo Schemidt)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Trump says he has 'no problem' with Russian oil tanker bringing relief to Cuba despite blockade
item.description: President Donald Trump said he has "no problem" with a Russian oil tanker off the coast of Cuba delivering relief to the island, which has been brought to its knees by a U.S. oil blockade.
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump said he has "no problem" with a Russian oil tanker off the coast of Cuba delivering relief to the island, which has been brought to its knees by a U.S. oil blockade.

(Image credit: Ramon Espinosa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: He wants children's bikes made in the U.S.A. — and tariffs against his rivals
item.description: Nearly all the bicycles sold in the United States are made overseas. An Indiana company set out to change that — and it's seeking a push from the Trump administration's tariffs.
item.content:encoded:
Nearly all the bicycles sold in the United States are made overseas. An Indiana company set out to change that — and it's seeking a push from the Trump administration's tariffs.

(Image credit: Scott Horsley)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: What's fueling all of the debt for the federal government?
item.description: The federal debt continues to grow, topping $39 trillion this month. Like a shopper who buys more than he earns every week, the nation's credit card bill is compounding. Just paying the interest now costs more than every other government program except Social Security.
item.content:encoded: The federal debt continues to grow, topping $39 trillion this month. Like a shopper who buys more than he earns every week, the nation's credit card bill is compounding. Just paying the interest now costs more than every other government program except Social Security.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: 'Fuel hedging' once kept airline prices down. Now passengers bear the brunt
item.description: Airlines used to do some financial magic to keep airfare down as oil prices increased, a strategy called "fuel hedging." But they stopped. Now fliers are on the hook for a lot of the difference.
item.content:encoded: Airlines used to do some financial magic to keep airfare down as oil prices increased, a strategy called "fuel hedging." But they stopped. Now fliers are on the hook for a lot of the difference.
item.dc:creator: Wailin Wong

item.title: War with Iran disrupts fertilizer exports as U.S. farmers prepare for planting season
item.description: Gulf states are major fertilizer producers, and the war with Iran has triggered a 25% price hike just as struggling U.S. farmers are planting corn.
item.content:encoded:
Gulf states are major fertilizer producers, and the war with Iran has triggered a 25% price hike just as struggling U.S. farmers are planting corn.

(Image credit: Frank Morris)
item.dc:creator: Frank Morris

item.title: They gave her business a lifeline, then froze all her money
item.description: A murky corner of the financial world is now the fastest-growing source of funding for small businesses. One state, Connecticut, had given these lenders unusual power. That may be about to change.
item.content:encoded: A murky corner of the financial world is now the fastest-growing source of funding for small businesses. One state, Connecticut, had given these lenders unusual power. That may be about to change.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: Trump delivers farmers another financial blow with Iran war
item.description: Some of President Trump's policies, the latest being the war in Iran, are testing his support among farmers who are being burdened with higher costs.
item.content:encoded:
Some of President Trump's policies, the latest being the war in Iran, are testing his support among farmers who are being burdened with higher costs.

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben

item.title: War with Iran disrupts fertilizer exports as US farmers prepare for planting season
item.description: Gulf states are major fertilizer producers and the war with Iran has triggered a 25% price hike, just as struggling U.S. farmers are planting corn.
item.content:encoded: Gulf states are major fertilizer producers and the war with Iran has triggered a 25% price hike, just as struggling U.S. farmers are planting corn.
item.dc:creator: Frank Morris
